title: Subtitles in EoE
wikitext:
[]: Subtitles in EoE             Quote Post by Squirrel Ninja » Fri May 30, 2008 3:43 pm So, I just finished watching eva for the first time in Japanese with English subs. For the record, I've got the platinum collection, and the movie boxed set, so those are the subs I'm referring to. I liked seeing the differences between the English dub translations and the sub translations (the subs were better in this respect). In EoE, however the subs were almost the same as the dubbed dialogs. Did the dub team take more ques from the sub guys, or did they just use the dub dialog as subs?

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Fri May 30, 2008 4:35 pm Probably the second... That's pretty common if they want to save money. I'm more of a Dub fan myself. I like to watch the movie, not read it.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Fri May 30, 2008 5:02 pm I'm pretty sure EoE had dubtitles.

[]:             Quote Post by Squirrel Ninja » Fri May 30, 2008 5:03 pm Which begs the question, I've seen the movie quoted around here with language not in the dub version, is there a popular fansub?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Fri May 30, 2008 5:11 pm a lot of downloaded versions have individually made subtitles (usually those that have acknowledgments at the beginning)

[]:             Quote Post by Squirrel Ninja » Fri May 30, 2008 5:24 pm I figured as much, but I wanted to make sure there wasn't any sort of "offical sub" or something like that floating around.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Fri May 30, 2008 5:28 pm WOW...this far without an AWL tirade. You're getting better, EGF. On, topic, I can't even watch my manga DVD's anymore. I haven't seen it in a good while, but i prefer the renewel fansub.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri May 30, 2008 5:41 pm It only warrants a tirade of someone claims her work was godly (shudders).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri May 30, 2008 8:41 pm Yes, they were dubtitles. In several spots of the film (excluding the final line and Misato's line about the Angels), it's clear lines from the dub are being used instead of what would actually translate from Japanese, if you know a bit of the language.

[]:             Quote Post by Squirrel Ninja » Fri May 30, 2008 9:06 pm Yeah I know some, and I noticed that as well, I'll check out the stuff on the net, thanks to all.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat May 31, 2008 1:54 am Squirrel Ninja wrote: is there a popular fansub?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat May 31, 2008 2:36 am ZapalacX wrote: WOW...this far without an AWL tirade. You're getting better, EGF. On, topic, I can't even watch my manga DVD's anymore. I haven't seen it in a good while, but i prefer the renewel fansub.

[]:             Quote Post by Squirrel Ninja » Sat May 31, 2008 8:45 am Yeah Manga's version is the only comercialy available DVD, of EoE; it wasn't in the platinum collection. Renewal's fansub would be internet territory.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat May 31, 2008 8:59 am Squirrel Ninja wrote: Yeah Manga's version is the only comercialy available DVD, of EoE; it wasn't in the platinum collection. Renewal's fansub would be internet territory.

[]: Subtitles in EoE             Quote Post by Squirrel Ninja » Fri May 30, 2008 3:43 pm So, I just finished watching eva for the first time in Japanese with English subs. For the record, I've got the platinum collection, and the movie boxed set, so those are the subs I'm referring to. I liked seeing the differences between the English dub translations and the sub translations (the subs were better in this respect). In EoE, however the subs were almost the same as the dubbed dialogs. Did the dub team take more ques from the sub guys, or did they just use the dub dialog as subs?

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Fri May 30, 2008 4:35 pm Probably the second... That's pretty common if they want to save money. I'm more of a Dub fan myself. I like to watch the movie, not read it.
